reasoning_content: We need to determine if 263 is prime. Check divisibility by small primes: 2: no, odd. 3: sum digits 2+6+3=11, not divisible by 3. 5: last digit not 0 or 5. 7: 7*37=259, 7*38=266, so 263-259=4, not divisible. 11: 11*23=253, 11*24=264, so not. 13: 13*20=260, remainder 3, not. 17: 17*15=255, remainder 8; 17*16=272 too high. 19: 19*13=247, remainder 16; 19*14=266. 23: 23*11=253, remainder 10; 23*12=276. 29: 29*9=261, remainder 2; 29*10=290. 31: 31*8=248, remainder 15; 31*9=279. 37: 37*7=259, remainder 4; 37*8=296. Next prime is 41, but sqrt(263) is about 16.2, so we only need to check primes up to 13? Actually sqrt(263) ≈ 16.22, so we need to check primes up to 13 (since 17 > sqrt). We've checked 2,3,5,7,11,13. None divide. So 263 is prime. Answer yes.
